__comment: CardDemo-Monitoring CloudWatch dashboard body. Provides unified observability across the two cloud-native workload types: (1) AWS Glue 5.1 PySpark batch jobs replacing the z/OS JES2/JCL batch pipeline (source JCL: app/jcl/POSTTRAN.jcl, app/jcl/INTCALC.jcl, app/jcl/COMBTRAN.jcl, app/jcl/CREASTMT.JCL, app/jcl/TRANREPT.jcl) and (2) the FastAPI API service on AWS ECS Fargate replacing the CICS region. This file replaces the z/OS SMF/SYSLOG batch-monitoring approach with AWS CloudWatch-based observability per AAP section 0.7.2 ('The system should be easy to monitor' and 'CloudWatch dashboard for unified observability'). Deploy with: aws cloudwatch put-dashboard --dashboard-name CardDemo-Monitoring --dashboard-body file://infra/cloudwatch/dashboard.json. Before deployment, replace every 'PLACEHOLDER' token with the deployment-specific ALB and target-group identifiers (see each widget's properties.metrics for locations). Widget layout on the 24-column grid: Glue metrics occupy y=0-11 (widgets 1-4) and ECS/ALB metrics occupy y=12-29 (widgets 5-9), with no overlapping widgets. Section grouping is documented via __section and __comment fields on each widget since the CloudWatch dashboard-body JSON schema forbids non-standard top-level keys but tolerates unknown widget-level properties.
__replaces: z/OS SMF/SYSLOG monitoring + CICS region console

widgets.__comment: Widget 1 of 9: Glue Job Duration. Tracks elapsed wall-clock time per Glue job execution. Source metric: glue.driver.aggregate.elapsedTime (built-in Glue metric emitted by every Glue job driver to the 'Glue' namespace). Displays all 5 pipeline stage jobs as separate lines to visualize per-stage runtime trends. Replaces z/OS step elapsed-time reporting from SMF type 30 records. Source JCL: POSTTRAN.jcl (Stage 1), INTCALC.jcl (Stage 2), COMBTRAN.jcl (Stage 3), CREASTMT.JCL (Stage 4a), TRANREPT.jcl (Stage 4b).

Widget 2 of 9: Glue DPU / Task Completion. Tracks the cumulative number of completed Spark tasks per Glue job as a proxy for work throughput and DPU consumption. Source metric: glue.driver.aggregate.numCompletedTasks (built-in Glue metric). Summed over the period to show total tasks finished per stage.

Widget 3 of 9: Glue Job Failures. Tracks failed Spark task counts per Glue job as the primary error-rate signal. Source metric: glue.driver.aggregate.numFailedTasks (built-in Glue metric). Any non-zero value indicates a failed task that should be investigated in CloudWatch Logs for the corresponding job run. This is the cloud-native replacement for JCL COND parameter non-zero return-code failures (per CREASTMT.JCL COND=(0,NE) semantics).

Widget 4 of 9: Glue JVM Heap Usage (Run Status Indicator). Tracks maximum JVM driver heap used during each Glue job run as a proxy for memory health and run status. Source metric: glue.driver.jvm.heap.used (built-in Glue metric). Spikes or saturation indicate memory pressure; sudden drops to zero indicate job termination (succeeded, failed, or timeout).

properties.title: Glue Job Run Status (JVM Heap Used - Succeeded/Failed/Timeout)

widgets.__section: AWS ECS Fargate API Service Metrics
widgets.__comment: Widget 5 of 9: ECS API CPU Utilization. Tracks average CPU utilization percentage for the carddemo-api ECS service. Source metric: CPUUtilization in the AWS/ECS namespace. Dimensioned by ClusterName='carddemo-cluster' and ServiceName='carddemo-api' (matches infra/ecs-task-definition.json family). Baseline utilization target under normal load is 40-60%; sustained >80% should trigger autoscaling evaluation.

widgets.__section: AWS ECS Fargate API Service Metrics
widgets.__comment: Widget 6 of 9: ECS API Memory Utilization. Tracks average memory utilization percentage for the carddemo-api ECS service. Source metric: MemoryUtilization in the AWS/ECS namespace. Dimensioned by ClusterName and ServiceName. The task has 1024 MB memory reservation per infra/ecs-task-definition.json; sustained >85% may indicate memory pressure or a leak.

widgets.__section: AWS ECS Fargate API Service Metrics
widgets.__comment: Widget 7 of 9: ALB Request Count. Tracks total requests served by the Application Load Balancer target group for carddemo-api. Source metric: RequestCount in the AWS/ApplicationELB namespace. The TargetGroup and LoadBalancer dimension values contain 'PLACEHOLDER' substrings that MUST be replaced with the actual ALB and target-group identifiers at deployment time (e.g., replace 'targetgroup/carddemo-api-tg/PLACEHOLDER' with 'targetgroup/carddemo-api-tg/1234567890abcdef').

widgets.__section: AWS ECS Fargate API Service Metrics
widgets.__comment: Widget 8 of 9: ALB Error Rate (HTTP 4xx/5xx). Tracks client errors (4xx) and server errors (5xx) returned by ECS targets behind the ALB. Source metrics: HTTPCode_Target_4XX_Count and HTTPCode_Target_5XX_Count in the AWS/ApplicationELB namespace. Both metrics are displayed on the same chart for at-a-glance error-rate visibility. 'PLACEHOLDER' substrings in TargetGroup and LoadBalancer dimensions MUST be replaced at deployment time. Sustained 5xx errors should trigger an alarm.

widgets.__section: AWS ECS Fargate API Service Metrics
widgets.__comment: Widget 9 of 9: ECS Active Task Count. Tracks the number of running ECS tasks for the carddemo-api service using ECS Container Insights. Source metric: RunningTaskCount in the ECS/ContainerInsights namespace. Drops below desired count indicate deployment failures or task crashes; requires Container Insights to be enabled on the cluster.
